summary: DHH announces he gave a private presentation on the Ruby web framework he's been developing alongside Basecamp. The post is a brief teaser from early 2004, before Rails was publicly released. He shares that the presentation was given over brunch to a small group of colleagues. He notes the slides alone don't capture the full four-hour oral presentation but hints at an upcoming beta release. The post was later updated with a link to the official Ruby on Rails release.
key_points: Rails was developed in conjunction with building Basecamp, not as a standalone project
The framework was first shown to a small circle before any public release
DHH gave a four-hour presentation on what would become one of the most influential web frameworks
money_quotes: I gave a presentation today over brunch on that upcoming Ruby web-framework I've been developing in conjunction Basecamp.
Hopefully it won't be too long before I'm ready to announce a beta version of the thing.
key_insight: Ruby on Rails was born directly out of building Basecamp, presented first to a handful of people over brunch before changing web development forever.
